country_gaps.NG: FIDA Nigeria (https://fida.org.ng/) exists as the natural institutional counterpart but no PA-specific institutional publication or named-counsel-of-record verifiable within time budget; entry deferred.
country_gaps.EG: National Council for Women (NCW) is the standing women's-rights body; publicly critiqued 2016 draft expanding non-custodial parent access on women-safety grounds; no PA-specialist named family-law advocate verifiable from public sources within time budget.
country_gaps.ZW: Sadiqi v Muteswa [2020] ZWHHC 249 anchors jurisprudence (court notes PA / abuse-allegation debate) but no PA-specialist named-counsel-of-record verifiable within time budget.
country_gaps.UG: Zero PA-relevant primary-source material located within time budget. Honest finding: zero verifiable entries.
country_gaps.BW: Zero PA-relevant primary-source material located within time budget. Honest finding: zero verifiable entries.

data_quality_notes: Marc Haskins SC: Pretoria Bar member page returned 404 at verification; LinkedIn confirmed SC status; firm email haskins@lawcircle.co.za reported in a third-party search snippet but could not be confirmed against the bar's own register.

T.L.D v B.G case-name variants: SAFLII cites T.L.D v B.G [2023] ZAGPJHC 801 (13 July 2023); a parallel reasons judgment exists at [2023] ZAGPJHC 872 (4 August 2023); Law Library SA cites the same case as D, TE v G, B (015642/2022) [2023] ZAGPJHC 821 — all three refer to the same matter before Adams J.

Sandton-bar PA-specialist gap: T.L.D v B.G originated in Gauteng Local Division (Johannesburg) but named curator ad litem is Pretoria-Bar-based; verification of any Sandton-side counsel-of-record requires the unredacted court file beyond the public SAFLII headnote.
